evals.prompt: Generate API tests for a REST API with these endpoints: POST /api/v1/products (create), GET /api/v1/products (list with pagination), GET /api/v1/products/:id (get by ID), PUT /api/v1/products/:id (update), DELETE /api/v1/products/:id (delete). The API uses JWT auth with admin and user roles. Only admins can create/update/delete. Use Supertest with NestJS.
evals.expected_output: Complete E2E test file covering all 7 test categories (CRUD, auth, validation, errors, pagination, rate limiting, authorization). Role-based tests for admin vs user. Error response format validation.

assertions.name: covers_all_endpoints
assertions.description: Tests exist for all 5 endpoints (POST, GET list, GET by ID, PUT, DELETE)

assertions.name: tests_auth
assertions.description: 401 tests for unauthenticated requests on protected endpoints

assertions.name: tests_authorization
assertions.description: 403 tests showing regular users cannot create/update/delete

assertions.name: tests_validation
assertions.description: 400 tests for missing/invalid fields on POST and PUT

assertions.name: tests_not_found
assertions.description: 404 test for non-existent product ID

assertions.name: tests_pagination
assertions.description: Tests for page/limit params and response meta

assertions.name: consistent_error_format
assertions.description: Error responses validated for standard format (statusCode, error, message)

evals.prompt: Generate tests for a GraphQL API built with Apollo Server on NestJS. Queries: users(filter, pagination), user(id). Mutations: createUser(input), updateUser(id, input), deleteUser(id). Auth via Bearer JWT, admin role required for delete. Use supertest sending GraphQL query strings to /graphql.
evals.expected_output: Tests sending GraphQL operations via POST /graphql with supertest, covering queries, mutations, auth, validation, and error responses

assertions.name: tests_graphql_queries
assertions.description: Tests send GraphQL query strings for users and user(id)

assertions.name: tests_graphql_mutations
assertions.description: Tests send GraphQL mutations for create, update, delete

assertions.name: tests_auth_on_mutations
assertions.description: Mutations without JWT return authentication error in GraphQL errors array

assertions.name: tests_input_validation
assertions.description: Invalid input (missing email, bad format) returns validation errors

assertions.name: tests_not_found
assertions.description: Query for non-existent user ID returns appropriate error

assertions.name: tests_pagination_args
assertions.description: Users query tested with first/after or page/limit arguments

evals.prompt: Generate tests for a file upload/download API on NestJS. POST /api/files/upload accepts multipart form data (single file, max 10MB, jpg/png/pdf only). GET /api/files/:id/download returns a presigned S3 URL. DELETE /api/files/:id removes file. JWT auth required on all endpoints. Use supertest.
evals.expected_output: Tests with multipart uploads via supertest .attach(), file size and type validation, presigned URL download, deletion, and auth checks

assertions.name: tests_successful_upload
assertions.description: Test uploads a valid file and asserts 201 with file ID in response

assertions.name: tests_file_size_rejection
assertions.description: Test sends file >10MB and asserts 400 or 413

assertions.name: tests_invalid_type_rejection
assertions.description: Test uploads .exe or .txt and asserts rejection

assertions.name: tests_download_url
assertions.description: Test GET /files/:id/download returns a URL string

assertions.name: tests_auth_required
assertions.description: Request without JWT returns 401

assertions.name: tests_delete
assertions.description: DELETE removes file and subsequent GET returns 404

evals.prompt: Generate tests for a multi-tenant SaaS API. Tenants identified by X-Tenant-ID header. Endpoints: GET /api/projects, POST /api/projects, GET /api/projects/:id. Tenant A must NEVER see Tenant B's data. JWT auth includes a tenantId claim that must match the header. NestJS + Supertest.
evals.expected_output: Tests verifying strict tenant isolation, cross-tenant access rejection, missing tenant header handling, CRUD within a single tenant, and auth enforcement

assertions.name: tests_tenant_isolation
assertions.description: Creates data as Tenant A, then verifies GET as Tenant B returns empty or excludes it

assertions.name: tests_cross_tenant_rejection
assertions.description: Tenant B trying to GET /projects/:id of Tenant A's project returns 403 or 404

assertions.name: tests_missing_tenant_header
assertions.description: Request without X-Tenant-ID header returns 400 or 403

assertions.name: tests_crud_within_tenant
assertions.description: Create and list projects within the same tenant works correctly

assertions.name: tests_auth_required
assertions.description: Unauthenticated request returns 401

assertions.name: consistent_error_format
assertions.description: Error responses use standard format with statusCode and message

evals.prompt: Generate tests for a Stripe webhook receiver at POST /api/webhooks/stripe. It validates the Stripe-Signature header using stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(). Handles events: checkout.session.completed, invoice.paid, charge.failed. Must be idempotent — processing the same event ID twice must not duplicate side effects. Returns 200 on success, 400 on invalid signature, 200 on unknown event types (acknowledge but ignore). NestJS + Supertest.
evals.expected_output: Tests with mocked Stripe signature construction, tests per event type, idempotency test, invalid signature rejection, unknown event handling

assertions.name: tests_valid_signature
assertions.description: Constructs a valid Stripe signature and asserts 200

assertions.name: tests_invalid_signature
assertions.description: Sends wrong signature and asserts 400

assertions.name: tests_each_event_type
assertions.description: Separate test for checkout.session.completed, invoice.paid, and charge.failed

assertions.name: tests_idempotency
assertions.description: Sends same event ID twice and verifies no duplicate processing

assertions.name: tests_unknown_event
assertions.description: Sends an unhandled event type and asserts 200 (acknowledged, not an error)

assertions.name: tests_returns_200_on_success
assertions.description: All valid webhook deliveries return 200 status
